question_text: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
clinical_definition: Diminished interest or pleasure in usual activities nearly every day for at least two weeks
natural_language_patterns: I do not enjoy anything anymore;Nothing feels fun;I stopped caring about my hobbies;I do not feel like doing anything;Things I used to love feel meaningless;I still do the things I usually like, but they dont feel quite as exciting lately;Some days Im just not as interested in my hobbies as I used to be;I can still enjoy things, it just takes me a bit longer to get into them;Ive noticed I sometimes have to push myself to start activities I used to jump into;Things are still okay, but I feel a little less enthusiastic than before;I still hang out with people, though Im not always really in the mood; I enjoy things sometimes, but not as consistently as I used to;Its not that Ive lost interest completely, just that it comes and goes;Some days Id rather skip activities I normally enjoy; I still like doing things, I just dont feel as excited about them; Ive definitely lost interest in some of the things I used to enjoy; I still try to do activities, but they dont feel very satisfying anymore;A lot of the time I just dont feel like doing things I used to like;Ive noticed my hobbies dont really make me happy the way they used to; I often skip activities because they just dont seem appealing anymore;Even when I go out or do something fun, it feels kind of flat;I find it hard to get motivated to do things I once enjoyed;I keep thinking I should do something I like, but I rarely follow through;I feel pretty indifferent toward most activities lately;Things that used to be fun now feel more like a chore;I dont really enjoy anything anymore;Everything that used to make me happy feels pointless now; Ive stopped doing most of the things I used to love;Even when I try to do something enjoyable, I feel nothing;I cant remember the last time something actually felt fun;Nothing excites me anymore, it all feels empty;I have no interest in hobbies or activities at all these days;Its like the pleasure has completely disappeared from my life; I avoid activities because I know I wont enjoy them anyway;I just dont feel any interest in anything anymore
functional_impact_examples: I stopped going out;I avoid social activities;I no longer participate in hobbies;I stay isolated at home;I lost motivation for daily tasks
severity_indicators: nearly every day;most of the day;persistent;lack of motivation;lasting for weeks
denial_patterns: No, I still enjoy my usual activities just fine;I dont think Ive lost interest in things at all;I still look forward to doing my hobbies;My interests are pretty much the same as always;I still enjoy spending time with people and doing things;Nothing has really changed in terms of what I like doing;I still get pleasure from the things I normally do;I havent noticed any drop in my interest in activities;Im still motivated to do the things I enjoy;Id say my enjoyment of activities is completely normal

clinical_definition: Persistent sadness low mood or hopelessness nearly every day for at least two weeks
natural_language_patterns: I feel down all the time;I feel empty;Everything feels hopeless;It feels heavy inside;I cannot see things getting better;Ive been feeling a little down some days, but it usually passes;Sometimes my mood is lower than usual, though I can still get through the day;Ive noticed I feel a bit sad more often lately;There are moments when I feel kind of gloomy, but its not all the time;I feel a little off emotionally some days;Ive had a few days where I just felt a bit blue for no clear reason;My mood hasnt been great lately, but its still manageable;I sometimes wake up feeling a little down, though it fades later;I feel slightly discouraged at times;Some days I just feel a bit low, even if nothing bad happened;Ive been feeling sad most days lately;My mood has been pretty low for a while now;I often feel down even when things are going okay;Its been hard to shake this feeling of sadness;I feel discouraged about a lot of things these days;I wake up already feeling kind of hopeless about the day;Ive been feeling emotionally heavy most of the time;I feel down more days than not lately;Even small things can make me feel overwhelmed or sad;It feels like a cloud over my mood most of the time;I feel sad almost all the time;Its like this constant feeling of sadness that wont go away;I feel hopeless about pretty much everything;Most days I just feel empty and deeply down;I cant remember the last time I actually felt happy;I feel like nothing is going to get better;The sadness is there every day, no matter what I do;I wake up feeling miserable and it lasts all day;I feel completely overwhelmed by how low my mood is;It feels like Im stuck in this dark place emotionally
functional_impact_examples: It is hard to get through the day;I struggle to complete responsibilities;I withdraw from others;My mood affects my work or study
severity_indicators: nearly every day;most of the day;chronic sadness;lasting weeks
denial_patterns: No, I wouldnt say Ive been feeling sad or down;My mood has been pretty normal lately;I dont really feel depressed or anything like that;Ive actually been feeling fine emotionally;I wouldnt describe myself as sad these days;My mood has been stable, nothing unusual;I still feel positive about things most of the time;I havent been feeling low or hopeless;Emotionally I feel about the same as usual;Im generally in a good mood overall

question_text: Trouble falling or staying asleep or sleeping too much
clinical_definition: Insomnia or hypersomnia occurring frequently during depressive episodes
natural_language_patterns: I cannot sleep at night;I wake up many times;I sleep too much;My sleep schedule is broken;I feel exhausted after sleeping;Ive had a few nights where it took me longer than usual to fall asleep;Sometimes I wake up earlier than I want to, but its not every night;My sleep has been a little off lately, but its still mostly okay;There are nights when I toss and turn for a while before falling asleep;Ive noticed my sleep isnt as restful as it used to be;Occasionally I wake up in the middle of the night and take a bit to fall back asleep;Ive had a few nights where I didnt sleep as well as I normally do;Some mornings I wake up feeling like I didnt get quite enough rest;My sleep schedule has been a little irregular recently;Ive been having the occasional restless night;Ive been having trouble falling asleep most nights lately;I wake up several times during the night and its hard to get back to sleep;My sleep has definitely been worse than usual;I often lie awake for a long time before finally falling asleep;Even when I sleep, I wake up feeling tired;Ive been sleeping a lot more than usual and still feel exhausted;Some nights I barely get a few hours of sleep;I keep waking up in the early morning and cant go back to sleep;My sleep feels really disrupted most nights;I either sleep too much or hardly at all lately;I barely sleep at all most nights;Im awake for hours and sometimes dont sleep until morning;My sleep is completely messed up right now;I wake up constantly during the night and never feel rested;I feel exhausted because I almost never get proper sleep;Some nights I dont sleep at all;I either stay awake all night or sleep nearly the entire day;Im so tired but my body just wont let me sleep;Even when I sleep, it feels like I didnt sleep at all;My sleep has gotten so bad that its affecting everything during the day
functional_impact_examples: I miss work because of sleep;I cannot wake up on time;I nap excessively;I feel tired during daily activities
denial_patterns: No, my sleep has been pretty normal;I usually fall asleep without any problems;I sleep through the night just fine;I feel well rested when I wake up;My sleep routine hasnt really changed;I get about the same amount of sleep as usual;I dont have trouble falling or staying asleep;Id say my sleep is actually quite good;I wake up feeling refreshed most mornings;Sleeping hasnt been an issue for me at all

question_text: Feeling tired or having little energy
clinical_definition: Persistent fatigue or reduced energy nearly every day
natural_language_patterns: I am tired all the time;I have no energy;Getting out of bed feels impossible;I feel drained;Even small tasks exhaust me;Ive been feeling a bit more tired than usual lately;Some days I feel a little low on energy, but I can still get things done;I notice I get tired more quickly than before;I feel a bit sluggish in the mornings sometimes;I still manage my day, but I dont feel as energetic as I used to;Occasionally I feel like I need more rest during the day;I feel slightly drained after normal activities;Some afternoons I just feel a little worn out;Ive been needing a bit more downtime lately;My energy isnt quite what it used to be;I feel tired most days, even when I sleep enough;It takes a lot more effort to get through the day;I feel drained even after doing simple things;I often feel like I have very little energy;I get exhausted much faster than I used to;Some days its hard to get started because I feel so tired;I feel worn out most of the time lately;Even small tasks can leave me feeling exhausted;I wake up already feeling tired;My energy levels are pretty low most days;I feel exhausted almost all the time;I barely have the energy to get out of bed;Everything feels physically draining to me;Im so tired that even basic tasks feel overwhelming;I feel completely worn out every day;I dont have the energy to do much of anything;It feels like all my energy is gone;I struggle to do even the simplest things because Im so tired;I feel physically and mentally exhausted all the time;Most days I just dont have the strength to do anything
functional_impact_examples: I cannot finish tasks;I fall behind at work;I skip responsibilities;I struggle with daily chores
denial_patterns: No, I actually feel about as energetic as usual;My energy levels have been pretty normal;I dont feel unusually tired;I can get through the day without feeling exhausted;I feel like I have plenty of energy most days;I havent noticed any drop in my energy;I feel physically fine and not fatigued;My energy levels are about the same as always;I dont feel drained or overly tired;Id say my energy is completely normal

clinical_definition: Significant decrease or increase in appetite during depressive episodes
natural_language_patterns: I barely eat;Food does not taste good;I eat more than usual;I forget to eat;I binge without thinking;My appetites been a little off lately, some days Im not that hungry;Ive noticed I skip meals sometimes without really thinking about it;Im eating a bit less than usual these days;Some days I just dont feel very hungry, but I still eat something;My appetite goes up and down more than it used to;I still eat regularly, just maybe smaller portions;Food doesnt always sound as appealing as it normally does;I occasionally forget to eat when Im busy or not in the mood;Ive been snacking a bit more than usual lately;My eating habits have changed a little, but nothing extreme;I often dont feel like eating even when I know I should;Ive definitely been eating much less than before;Some days I only eat because I know I have to;Food just doesnt taste as good or appealing anymore;I find myself skipping meals pretty often;Ive been overeating some days and barely eating on others;My appetite has dropped quite a bit lately;I notice Im eating way more than usual, almost without thinking;Sometimes I realize I havent eaten much the whole day;My eating habits feel pretty different from what they used to be;I hardly eat at all most days;I have almost no appetite anymore;I sometimes go the whole day without eating;Food doesnt interest me at all right now;I feel like forcing myself to eat is really difficult;Ive been eating way too much and cant seem to stop;I either eat almost nothing or binge eat a lot;My appetite feels completely out of control;Even when food is in front of me, I dont want it;My eating has changed so much that its worrying me
functional_impact_examples: I lose weight unintentionally;I gain weight;My eating habits affect my routine;I skip meals
denial_patterns: No, my appetite has been pretty normal;Im eating about the same as I usually do;I still enjoy food and meals like before;My eating habits havent really changed;I feel hungry at normal times during the day;I havent noticed any difference in how much I eat;Im still eating regular meals without any problems;Food still tastes good and I enjoy eating;My appetite feels completely normal to me;I havent had any issues with eating or appetite

question_text: Feeling bad about yourself or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
clinical_definition: Feelings of worthlessness excessive guilt or self blame
natural_language_patterns: I feel like a failure;Everything is my fault;I do not deserve happiness;I let everyone down;I feel worthless;Sometimes I feel like I should be doing better than I am;I catch myself being a bit hard on myself lately;I occasionally feel like Im not doing enough;I sometimes think I could have handled things better;I feel a little guilty about small mistakes more than I used to;I get the feeling Im letting people down sometimes;Ive been doubting myself more than usual;Sometimes I feel like Im not quite measuring up;I notice I blame myself when things dont go well;I feel a bit disappointed in myself at times;I often feel like Im not good enough;I blame myself for things even when they might not be my fault;I keep thinking Im letting people down;I feel like Im not really worth much lately;Im pretty critical of myself most of the time;I feel guilty about things that probably arent a big deal;I keep replaying mistakes in my head;I feel like Im a burden to other people sometimes;I feel like Im failing at a lot of things;I often think Im not as capable as others;I feel completely worthless most of the time;I honestly feel like Im a burden to everyone around me;I blame myself for almost everything that goes wrong;I feel like I dont deserve good things in my life;I think people would be better off without me;I feel like Ive failed at everything that matters;I constantly feel guilty even when I havent done anything wrong;I feel like theres something fundamentally wrong with me;I cant stop thinking about how useless I am;I feel like I have no value at all
functional_impact_examples: I avoid people because I feel ashamed;I cannot accept praise;I isolate due to guilt;persistent self blame
denial_patterns: No, I dont feel worthless or anything like that;I generally feel okay about myself;I dont really blame myself for things unnecessarily;I feel like Im doing alright in life;I dont feel excessive guilt about things;Im fairly confident in myself most of the time;I wouldnt say I feel like a burden to others;I feel like I have value and contribute to things;I dont spend much time criticizing myself;Overall I feel pretty comfortable with who I am

question_text: Trouble concentrating on things such as reading or watching television
clinical_definition: Reduced ability to think focus or concentrate
natural_language_patterns: I cannot focus;My mind goes blank;I forget things easily;I lose track of conversations;I feel mentally slow;Sometimes I find myself losing focus for a bit, but I can usually get back on track;Ive noticed I get distracted a little more easily lately;It sometimes takes me a bit longer to concentrate on things;I occasionally lose my train of thought during conversations;I have to reread things once in a while to really understand them;My focus isnt as sharp as it used to be on some days;I get distracted when Im working, but I can still finish tasks;Sometimes I catch myself zoning out for a moment;I have a little trouble staying focused if something takes a long time;I feel like my attention drifts sometimes;I have a hard time concentrating on tasks most days;I often lose track of what Im doing halfway through something;Reading or focusing on work has become pretty difficult lately;I find myself forgetting what I just read or heard;Its hard to stay focused even on simple tasks;I keep getting distracted and it takes effort to refocus;I struggle to follow conversations sometimes;My mind feels foggy when I try to concentrate;I often start something but cant stay focused long enough to finish;Even small tasks require a lot of effort to focus on;I can barely concentrate on anything anymore;My mind feels so foggy that its hard to think clearly;I struggle to focus even for a few minutes;I cant follow what people are saying sometimes;Reading or watching something is almost impossible for me now;I lose track of thoughts constantly;It feels like my brain just wont focus at all;I cant stay focused long enough to finish even simple things;My concentration is so bad that its affecting everything I do;I feel mentally overwhelmed whenever I try to focus
functional_impact_examples: I struggle to complete assignments;I make mistakes at work;I fall behind academically;I cannot follow tasks
denial_patterns: No, my concentration has been pretty normal;I can focus on things without any real problems;I havent noticed any issues with my attention;Im able to think clearly and stay focused;My concentration feels about the same as usual;I can read or work without losing focus;I dont have trouble paying attention to things;My mind feels clear when Im doing tasks;I can follow conversations easily;Id say my ability to concentrate is completely normal

question_text: Moving or speaking slowly or being restless
clinical_definition: Psychomotor agitation or retardation observable by others
natural_language_patterns: I feel slowed down;I move very slowly;I cannot sit still;I feel restless inside;People notice I act differently;Ive noticed I move a bit slower than usual sometimes;Some days I feel a little restless and cant sit still for long;I feel like my pace has slowed down a bit lately;I catch myself fidgeting more than I used to;It sometimes takes me longer to get started on things;I feel a little sluggish when Im doing daily tasks;Occasionally I feel slightly restless without knowing why;My movements feel a bit slower on some days;I sometimes tap my foot or move around more than usual;I feel a little less physically active than before;I feel noticeably slower when I move or do things;People have mentioned that I seem a bit sluggish lately;It takes a lot longer for me to get moving in the morning;I often feel restless and cant stay still;My body feels heavy and slow most days;I pace around sometimes because I feel uneasy;Simple movements feel like they take more effort;I feel like everything I do is in slow motion;Others have noticed I seem either restless or slowed down;I struggle to keep up with my usual pace;I move extremely slowly most of the time;It feels like my body is stuck in slow motion;I barely have the energy to move around;People constantly tell me I seem very slowed down;I feel so restless that I cant stop pacing;My movements feel extremely heavy and difficult;Even small physical actions feel exhausting;I cant sit still at all, I feel constantly agitated;I feel either completely slowed down or extremely restless;My body just doesnt respond the way it used to
functional_impact_examples: I take longer to complete tasks;Others comment on my behavior;I feel physically uncomfortable;I pace around frequently
denial_patterns: No, my movements feel completely normal;I havent noticed any changes in how fast I move;I dont feel restless or slowed down;My energy and physical pace feel the same as usual;I move around just like I normally do;No one has mentioned anything unusual about my movements;I feel comfortable sitting still or being active when needed;My body feels normal in terms of movement;I havent felt unusually restless or sluggish;Everything about my physical activity feels typical
